Then there's a section about the "Larger Than Life" trend being a favorite, emphasizing big toys with big play value. Then there's a part about education with STEAM (science, technology, engineering, art, and math) and mention of word games and high-tech games. The article also says that technology is always going to influence toys, and this year they saw 3D games and robots. However, it doesn't explicitly say robots are the favorite of buyers, just that they were present. Looking at the options: A) Toys are becoming more educational than fun. The text does mention a push towards education with STEAM, but it doesn't say they're becoming more educational *than fun*. It's a push, but not necessarily a shift. B) Robots are great favorites of buyers. The text mentions robots as part of the technology trend, but "great favorites" isn't directly stated. It's possible, but maybe not the best inference. C) Big toys will be popular this year. The text clearly states that "Larger Than Life" was a favorite trend, and oversize toys were highlighted. This seems like a solid inference. D) Simple card games will disappear on the market. The text mentions that simple card games were shown, but there's no indication they will disappear.
